creator: eb30750@gmail.com
text:
From the gnome interface, I logged in as root and open up Firefox and a few file system windows.  I then invoked the logout menu but instead of logging out I performed a switch user.  I then logged in with my personal account and password.  I performed some tasks there and decided to go back to the root gnome session.  I again invoked the logout menu from this session and selected the switch user menu item again.  This time the screen went white and presented a small dialog box stating "There is a problem with the configuration server."
"..... gconf-sanity-check-2 exited with 134"

I Then logged back in using my personal account and then logged out terminating this session.  I was returned to my initial root session.

Fix: 

This might constitute an improper use of switch user.  I would think one could easily switch between user sessions.  If not, then there should be a more informative error message.
